meaning: adj.受教育不足的,未受良好教育的
examples: Residents in general are poor and undereducated, and live in substandard housing.
Precious is so undereducated that she can not read and write formally.
On JANUARY 13th Gordon Brown's government announced its latest attempt to improve the chances of the poor and undereducated.
Undereducated and generally unemployed, they have seen no peace dividend and feel little identification with the political process or its participants.
In fact, the ways in which the credit card, insurance and collection industries take advantage of undereducated consumers should be the focus of your attention.
